skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Guides structured brainstorming before implementation by clarifying goals, comparing alternatives, and validating design decisions incrementally.
skill.summary: Refines rough ideas into validated designs before implementation begins.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static findings to be false positives from Markdown fences, sample product questions, and process prose. No executable code, credential access, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown code fences and examples. They document checklists, questions, and a DOT diagram; they do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are fenced Markdown delimiters or documentation examples. No executable script, shell invocation, or command interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Illustrative Browser Storage Example Is Not Credential Access
low_findings.description: The browser storage references are options in a sample product question. The skill does not read browser files, access credentials, or instruct data extraction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines only list local storage and cookies as design choices in a hypothetical question. There is no code path or instruction to inspect browser storage.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: The reported blocker matches occur in descriptive text about design, validation, and trade-offs. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption code appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows process language and headings, not cryptographic APIs. The duplicated static hits do not correspond to executable code.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection Is an Architecture Example
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance hit appears inside a sample architecture option. It describes background jobs as a design trade-off, not host inspection or system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a natural-language option in a questionnaire. It contains no command, API, or instruction to gather system information.

content.value_statement: Early product and engineering ideas often fail because requirements and trade-offs are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured questions, alternatives, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Guides one-question-at-a-time requirement discovery before implementation.
Frames two or three design approaches with trade-offs and complexity notes.
Uses structured choices when decisions have clear alternatives.
Presents designs in short sections for incremental user validation.
Defines when to revisit requirements or alternatives after new constraints appear.
Hands off approved designs to worktree setup and implementation planning workflows.
limitations: Does not implement code by itself; it prepares the design stage.
Depends on user answers for accurate constraints and success criteria.
References separate worktree and planning skills that may not be installed.
Does not replace domain expert review for regulated or high-risk decisions.

use_cases.title: Shape a Product Feature
use_cases.description: Clarify goals, user needs, and success criteria before choosing a feature direction.

use_cases.title: Compare Engineering Approaches
use_cases.description: Evaluate architecture options with trade-offs before writing an implementation plan.

use_cases.title: Validate a Project Idea
use_cases.description: Turn a rough concept into a structured design that stakeholders can review.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a Rough Idea
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner discovery for an early concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to help me refine this idea: [idea]. Ask one question at a time and identify the key constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Feature Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare practical alternatives before choosing a direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to explore two or three approaches for [feature]. Show trade-offs, complexity, and risks before recommending next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Technical Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a proposed architecture before planning implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to validate this design: [design]. Present it in sections and ask for feedback after each section.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Implementation Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handoff from approved design to planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to refine [project], compare alternatives, validate the final design, and prepare a planning handoff once approved.

output_examples.input: I want to add team invitations to our app.
output: Clarifying question about invitation scope and permissions.
Alternative flows for email invites, share links, or admin-only provisioning.
A validated design section covering roles, errors, and acceptance criteria.

output_examples.input: We need a better onboarding flow but only have a vague goal.
output: A short discovery sequence that identifies users, friction points, and success metrics.
Three possible onboarding approaches with trade-offs.
A concise recommendation ready for stakeholder review.

output_examples.input: Help me decide how to process background tasks.
output: A comparison of synchronous calls, queue-based processing, and hybrid jobs.
Risk and complexity notes for each approach.
A design summary that can feed into an implementation plan.

best_practices: Start with purpose, constraints, and success criteria before discussing solutions.
Use structured choices only when options are distinct and mutually exclusive.
Validate each design section before moving into implementation planning.
anti_patterns: Skipping discovery and asking the model to implement the first idea.
Presenting many options without clear trade-offs or a decision point.
Forcing a linear process when new constraints require revisiting earlier phases.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps turn rough ideas into structured, validated designs before implementation starts.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on discovery, alternatives, design validation, and planning handoff.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it before implementation, especially when requirements, trade-offs, or success criteria are unclear.

faq.question: Why does it ask one question at a time?
faq.answer: Single questions reduce ambiguity and make each decision easier to validate.

faq.question: Are the static security findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. AI review found they are false positives from Markdown examples and descriptive text.
